summary_one_line: Free a text/comment node (cur->type is XML_TEXT_NODE or XML_COMMENT_NODE) so the name-DICT_FREE guard takes its false side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must drive xmlFreeNode on a node whose type is XML_TEXT_NODE or XML_COMMENT_NODE, so the compound guard at tree.c:3891-3893 short-circuits false on the type check; this is reached when content-bearing text/comment nodes (with cur->content != NULL) are freed during parse/teardown rather than only element/DTD-subset nodes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx reaches the content-freeing else-if at lines 3880-3884 ([W] 3883 DICT_FREE(cur->content)) and at the blocker line 3893 takes the false side (T=0 F=3), meaning the freed node carried content and was a text/comment node; the context hook surfaced the xmlFreeDtd subset-cleanup call path (lines 1121-1132 all [W]) as new (call-context, edge) coverage that pulled the campaign into this teardown path.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the false side: at the blocker it only ever takes true (T=18 F=0) and never hits the content-free else-if (line 3883 is [L]-absent, line 3894 DICT_FREE(cur->name) is [L]); naive's bare-edge MaxMapFeedback gives no new-coverage credit for reaching xmlFreeNode through the text/comment-node call context, so it stalls (blocked 11.70h, 0 hits) freeing only name-bearing nodes.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same xmlFreeNode edge reached via the text/comment-node teardown path is a new (call-context, edge) bucket and earns a corpus-add; naive's plain per-edge bucket already saw that edge via name-node frees and gives no reward, so only the context-sensitive feedback dimension rewards exploring the false side.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of the comment-node type check inside xmlFreeNode's name-DICT_FREE guard.
The globally blocked side is the false branch.
The winner reaches the content-freeing else-if branch that precedes the name guard.
At the blocker line the winner takes the false side three times while the loser takes the true side eighteen times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L3893  T=0 F=3  T=18 F=0  (cur->type != XML_COMMENT_NODE))  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser only ever takes the name-bearing (true) path and reaches DICT_FREE(cur->name).
The winner exercised the xmlFreeDtd subset-cleanup call path that the loser did not.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx's new-coverage signal is a new (call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: The naive loser uses a plain per-edge hit-count bucket as its only corpus-add signal.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

evidence_trail.claim: naive resolved the branch in 0/10 trials and stayed blocked with zero hits while naive_ctx resolved 9/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=16  loser=0

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Claimed mechanism is context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx CtxHook), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory lineage check does not apply; verification is by source/coverage evidence: the per-role overlay shows the blocker false side is W-only (L3893 T=0 F=3 for W vs T=18 F=0 for L) and the winning seed (fuzzer=naive_ctx) carries only havoc mutation_op (ByteIncMutator,ByteInterestingMutator) with no I2S, consistent with a feedback-dimension rather than roadblock-dimension win.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only divergence axis is freeing a text/comment node behind a deeper call context: if plain naive (bare-edge feedback) resolves the false side as fast as naive_ctx, the context-sensitive-coverage attribution is refuted.

weakest_evidence_point: The link from new (call-context, edge) credit to specifically reaching text/comment-node frees is inferred from the W/L overlay; no seed metadata directly proves the context hook (not throughput luck) caused the corpus-add at this edge.
